title: GTC March 2025 Keynote with NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang
notes: Flagship industry keynote; dense technical claims about Blackwell/Rubin/AI factories with many quoted figures models will be tempted to fabricate

title: WWDC 2024 Keynote (Apple Intelligence reveal)
notes: Multi-presenter corporate keynote; tests speaker attribution and product naming precision (Apple Intelligence vs Apple AI etc.)

title: A Long Strange Loop (Strange Loop 2023 final keynote)
notes: Programming-language-theory community keynote; mixes technical references and historical narrative typical of dense conference talks

title: DEF CON 32 - Counter Deception: Defending Yourself in a World Full of Lies
notes: Security domain with technical acronyms and adversarial-thinking framework; jargon-heavy and easy to mis-summarise

title: [Lecon inaugurale] Yann Le Cun - Apprentissage profond et au-dela : les nouveaux defis de l'IA
channel: Ecole nationale des ponts et chaussees
notes: French academic keynote with deep-learning jargon spoken aloud; tests multilingual technical summarisation

title: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code, spelled out.
notes: Reference scientific course on transformer architecture; high pedagogy density per minute and well-known so any hallucination is easy to catch

title: Let's build the GPT Tokenizer
notes: Same instructor different topic (BPE tokenization); pairs with sc-01 to test whether models conflate the two lectures

title: L'apprentissage profond : une revolution en intelligence artificielle - Yann LeCun (2016)
notes: French academic flagship; deep-learning content from 2016 era so models trained on 2024+ data will be tempted to retrofit modern claims

title: Ce que peut l'histoire - Patrick Boucheron (2015)
notes: Inaugural lesson on historiography; non-STEM French academic register tests coverage on humanities content

title: La peste noire (1) - Patrick Boucheron (2020-2021)
channel: Histoire et archeologie - College de France
notes: Medieval history course with many dates and proper nouns; tests faithfulness on factual claims models cannot verify in the transcript

title: Andrej Karpathy: Tesla AI, Self-Driving, Optimus, Aliens, and AGI | Lex Fridman Podcast #333
notes: Long-form business+tech interview two speakers; tests speaker attribution at 3h+ duration

title: Yann Lecun: Meta AI, Open Source, Limits of LLMs, AGI & the Future of AI | Lex Fridman Podcast #416
notes: Pairs with bp-01 to test whether models distinguish between two AI-luminary interviews on the same podcast

title: Joe Rogan Experience #2195 - Andrew Huberman
notes: Highest-volume conversational podcast; tests actionability extraction on health/lifestyle claims models often hedge

title: 3h avec une LEGENDE du business (Generation Do It Yourself)
channel: Matthieu Stefani - Generation Do It Yourself
notes: French business podcast 3h; dense actionable claims and entrepreneurship vocabulary

title: Comprendre la guerre qui vient ? Henri Guaino [EN DIRECT] (Thinkerview)
notes: French opinionated interview 3h41; high hallucination risk on geopolitical claims and live-stream digressions

title: Deep Dive into LLMs like ChatGPT
notes: 3h31 procedural deep-dive on LLM training stack; tests step-ordering preservation on a complex pipeline

title: Learn Python - Full Course for Beginners [Tutorial]
notes: Canonical 4h26 beginner tutorial with code-on-screen; tests whether models invent optional steps not in the transcript

title: Docker and Kubernetes - Full Course for Beginners
notes: 4h17 procedural with two co-instructors; tests speaker switching and command-syntax fidelity (CLI flags are easy to fabricate)

title: Docker Tutorial for Beginners [FULL COURSE in 3 Hours]
notes: Pairs with ht-03 (same topic different instructor) to test whether models conflate the two tutorials when both are in the corpus

title: [1hr Talk] Intro to Large Language Models
notes: Shorter 59min general-audience walkthrough; lower bound for tutorial duration in the corpus to test compression on shorter inputs
